pattern: String validation requiring minimum length, alphanumeric-only characters, at least one vowel, and at least one consonant.
hints: Check all four conditions independently: length >= 3, only alphanumeric chars, at least one vowel, at least one consonant.
Use a set of vowels {'a','e','i','o','u'} and their uppercase counterparts; a consonant is a letter that is not in this set.
Iterate once through the string tracking has_vowel and has_consonant flags, then combine with the length and character-type checks.

edge_inputs.description: minimum valid word: 3 chars, vowel and consonant
all digits - no vowel or consonant
special char makes it invalid despite length and letters
uppercase vowel should count as vowel
digit in middle valid if other conditions met

stress.description: random alphanumeric and special char strings of varying length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    charset = 'abcdefghijklmnopqrstuvwxyzABCDEFGHIJKLMNOPQRSTUVWXYZ0123456789@#$'
    length = rng.randint(1, 20)
    w = ''.join(rng.choice(list(charset)) for _ in range(length))
    return [w]
